name: Point Zéro des Routes de France
resonance_reason: Every French road distance originates here; standing here collapses all geography into one point.
micro_ritual_hint: Stand on the star. Close your eyes. Feel all roads converge.
quiet_line: All roads begin beneath your feet.

name: Notre-Dame de Paris
resonance_reason: Built at the energetic center of the island-city; stone walls hold eight centuries of collective prayer.
micro_ritual_hint: Touch the portal stone before crossing the threshold.
quiet_line: The stone remembers every voice.

name: Sainte-Chapelle
resonance_reason: Fifteen windows dissolve solid walls into pure light; architecture as theological argument made visible.
micro_ritual_hint: Look upward before entering. Let the light arrive first.
quiet_line: Here, glass becomes the sky.

name: Tour Saint-Jacques
resonance_reason: Lone tower marking the departure point of the Santiago de Compostela pilgrimage route westward.
micro_ritual_hint: Face west at the base. Feel the pull of the road not taken.
quiet_line: Pilgrims left from here and never returned.

name: Panthéon
resonance_reason: Republican mausoleum where France buries its secular saints; the dome anchors the Left Bank skyline.
micro_ritual_hint: Pause at the crypt stairs. Descent here is an argument.
quiet_line: Greatness sleeps below the living.

name: Catacombes de Paris (Ossuary)
resonance_reason: Six million souls compressed into darkness beneath the city's busiest streets; inversion of the living Paris above.
micro_ritual_hint: Stop at the entrance inscription. Read it twice.
quiet_line: The city is built on the dead.

name: Sacré-Cœur Basilica
resonance_reason: Hilltop vigil site with perpetual adoration since consecration; city radiates below in all directions.
micro_ritual_hint: Sit on the parvis steps facing the city. Let Paris come to you.
quiet_line: From here the city kneels.

name: Arc de Triomphe
resonance_reason: Twelve avenues radiate from a single void; the eternal flame burns at the geometric heart of empire.
micro_ritual_hint: Stand at the center. Count the axes. You are the axis.
quiet_line: Power radiates from a single flame.

name: Arago Meridian Medallions
resonance_reason: 135 bronze discs mark Paris's prime meridian; an invisible north–south axis hiding in plain sight underfoot.
micro_ritual_hint: Find one medallion. Look north. The line continues without end.
quiet_line: A secret line bisects the city.

name: Observatoire de Paris
resonance_reason: Meridian zero before Greenwich; Paris once measured the world from this exact longitude.
micro_ritual_hint: Stand on the meridian line in the courtyard. Face north.
quiet_line: Paris once owned the center of the world.

name: Fontaine des Innocents
resonance_reason: Built over Paris's oldest cemetery; water flows eternally above a ground that held the city's dead for centuries.
micro_ritual_hint: Touch the fountain edge. The water has flowed since the medieval city.
quiet_line: Water rises from where the dead rested.

name: Colonne Vendôme
resonance_reason: Cast from 1,200 melted enemy cannons; the column is a vertical battlefield frozen in bronze spiral.
micro_ritual_hint: Circle it once. War is wound around this stone.
quiet_line: A thousand cannonballs became one monument.

name: Galerie Vivienne
resonance_reason: Mosaic floors and glass vaults create a sealed world that pre-dates the modern street; time stalls inside.
micro_ritual_hint: Enter from Rue Vivienne. Look down at the mosaic first, then up.
quiet_line: Rain cannot reach this street.

name: Colonnes de Buren, Palais-Royal
resonance_reason: Minimalist columns fracture a royal courtyard into a field of perception; order and play in perfect tension.
micro_ritual_hint: Walk the columns in silence. Each one changes the space behind you.
quiet_line: The courtyard argues with itself.

name: Musée Gustave Moreau
resonance_reason: Painter's private cosmos preserved intact; a mind made visible in every corner and staircase.
micro_ritual_hint: Climb the spiral stair slowly. Let the density of vision accumulate.
quiet_line: One man painted a universe here.

name: Église Saint-Sulpice
resonance_reason: Gnomon line traces winter solstice light across the floor; the sun becomes a liturgical instrument.
micro_ritual_hint: Find the copper meridian line on the floor. Follow it to the obelisk.
quiet_line: The sun enters and tells the time.

name: Père Lachaise Cemetery
resonance_reason: City of the dead mapped like a city of the living; here Paris rehearses its own memory.
micro_ritual_hint: Walk without a map. Let the city of the dead direct you.
quiet_line: The famous and forgotten share the same silence.

name: Arènes de Lutèce
resonance_reason: Roman amphitheater buried and forgotten for centuries; the oldest crowd-space in the city, now occupied by pétanque.
micro_ritual_hint: Sit on the stone tiers. Watch whoever occupies the arena now.
quiet_line: Gladiators played. Now neighbors do.

name: Place de la Bastille
resonance_reason: Where the prison stood, now a traffic circle; absence is the monument — the void marks rupture.
micro_ritual_hint: Find the outline of the old prison in the pavement. Stand inside it.
quiet_line: The most important building here no longer exists.

name: La Conciergerie
resonance_reason: Last address before the guillotine; the waiting rooms of revolution still hold their original silence.
micro_ritual_hint: Stand at the riverside facade at dusk. Count the towers. Three remain.
quiet_line: History passed through this gate screaming.

name: Église Saint-Germain-des-Prés
resonance_reason: Oldest church in Paris still standing; beneath the cafe noise, a Merovingian silence persists.
micro_ritual_hint: Enter the side door. Let your eyes adjust before looking up.
quiet_line: The oldest prayers in Paris live here.

name: Chapelle de la Médaille Miraculeuse
resonance_reason: Active place of apparition; pilgrims arrive daily to a site that resists secular time entirely.
micro_ritual_hint: Wait in the queue. Observe who comes and why.
quiet_line: Faith keeps this address off most maps.

name: Temple de la Sibylle, Buttes-Chaumont
resonance_reason: Folly perched on artificial cliff; the city disappears and reappears from here like a recurring dream.
micro_ritual_hint: Cross the suspension bridge alone. Look back at the temple from the far bank.
quiet_line: A temple built to hold a view.

name: La Petite Ceinture (15th section)
resonance_reason: Abandoned railway ring reclaimed by vegetation; nature's counter-city runs silently above the inhabited one.
micro_ritual_hint: Step onto the track. Notice which direction the silence comes from.
quiet_line: The train stopped. The city grew around its ghost.

name: Moulin de la Galette
resonance_reason: Last surviving windmill of Montmartre's hilltop skyline; Renoir stood exactly here to paint collective joy.
micro_ritual_hint: Find the spot where Renoir's perspective aligns. Stand still for sixty seconds.
quiet_line: A painter froze this light a century ago.

name: Passage Brady
resonance_reason: A 19th-century arcade colonized by South Asian restaurants; two epochs coexist in a single corridor of spices and glass.
micro_ritual_hint: Walk the full length without stopping. Smell what changes.
quiet_line: History smells like cardamom here.

name: Assemblée Nationale — Bibliothèque Delacroix
resonance_reason: Delacroix's ceiling presides over France's living law; mythology watches democracy in session.
micro_ritual_hint: Look up before looking at any document or debate below.
quiet_line: Gods painted the ceiling. Men argue below.

name: Square du Vert-Galant
resonance_reason: Westernmost tip of the Île de la Cité; the prow of Paris cuts the Seine at city's oldest inhabited ground.
micro_ritual_hint: Sit at the very tip. Let the river flow past both sides simultaneously.
quiet_line: The city begins here, in the water.
